paper_metadata.title: GLaM: Efficient Scaling of Language Models with Mixture-of-Experts
paper_metadata.abstract: Scaling language models with more data, compute and parameters has driven significant progress in natural language processing. For example, thanks to scaling, GPT-3 was able to achieve strong results on in-context learning tasks. However, training these large dense models requires significant amounts of computing resources. In this paper, we propose and develop a family of language models named GLaM (Generalist Language Model), which uses a sparsely activated mixture-of-experts architecture to scale the model capacity while also incurring substantially less training cost compared to dense variants. The largest GLaM has 1.2 trillion parameters, which is approximately 7x larger than GPT-3. It consumes only 1/3 of the energy used to train GPT-3 and requires half of the computation flops for inference, while still achieving better overall zero-shot and one-shot performance across 29 NLP tasks.

extraction_data.innovation: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture enabling sparse activation, where only a subset of experts (parameters) are activated per prediction, significantly improving computational efficiency and scalability compared to dense models. Achieves state-of-the-art performance on TriviaQA and other tasks with lower energy consumption than dense models like GPT-3.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Large-scale diverse text corpus (1.6T tokens from 28 sources including web pages, books, and Wikipedia)
extraction_data.extension: Scaling the number of experts in MoE layers to improve predictive performance for a fixed computational budget per prediction. Uses computation efficiency optimizations from TPU-v4 hardware and GSPMD software.
extraction_data.research_problem: Efficient scaling of large language models with reduced computational cost and energy consumption while maintaining or improving performance on few-shot learning tasks.
extraction_data.application: Zero-shot, one-shot, and few-shot learning across 29 NLP benchmarks, including language completion, open-domain QA, natural language inference, and common sense reasoning.
extraction_data.paper_title: GLaM: Efficient Scaling of Language Models with Mixture-of-Experts

extraction_data.innovation: Sparsely activated subnetwork of GLaM's 1.2T parameter model, where only 8% of parameters (96.6B) are activated per token, enabling efficient inference and training with reduced FLOPs and energy consumption.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Large-scale diverse text corpus (1.6T tokens from 28 sources including web pages, books, and Wikipedia)
extraction_data.extension: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) with 64 experts per MoE layer, enabling conditional computation where each token activates only a subset of parameters.
extraction_data.research_problem: Efficient scaling of large language models with reduced computational cost and energy consumption while maintaining or improving performance on few-shot learning tasks.
extraction_data.application: Zero-shot, one-shot, and few-shot learning across 29 NLP benchmarks, including language completion, open-domain QA, natural language inference, and common sense reasoning.
extraction_data.paper_title: GLaM: Efficient Scaling of Language Models with Mixture-of-Experts

extraction_data.innovation: Sparsely activated Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture, where each MoE layer dynamically selects the top-2 experts per token, enabling efficient scaling with significantly fewer activated parameters per token compared to dense models. Uses per-layer relative positional bias and Gated Linear Units (GLU) in feed-forward sub-layers.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: High-quality dataset of 1.6 trillion tokens (filtered webpages, Wikipedia, conversations, forums, books, news)
extraction_data.application: Few-shot learning, language understanding, text generation
extraction_data.research_problem: Efficient scaling of large language models with reduced computational cost during training and inference
extraction_data.extension: Replaces standard positional embeddings with per-layer relative positional bias and uses Gated Linear Units (GLU) in feed-forward sub-layers. The MoE layers are interleaved with Transformer layers, and weights are partitioned using a 2D sharding algorithm for large-scale training.
extraction_data.paper_title: GLaM: Efficient Scaling of Language Models with Mixture-of-Experts

extraction_data.innovation: Sparsely activated Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture, where each MoE layer dynamically selects the top-2 experts per token, enabling efficient scaling with significantly fewer activated parameters per token compared to dense models. Uses per-layer relative positional bias and Gated Linear Units (GLU) in feed-forward sub-layers.

extraction_data.application: Few-shot learning, language understanding, text generation
extraction_data.research_problem: Efficient scaling of large language models with reduced computational cost during training and inference
extraction_data.extension: Replaces standard positional embeddings with per-layer relative positional bias and uses Gated Linear Units (GLU) in feed-forward sub-layers. extraction_data.paper_title: GLaM: Efficient Scaling of Language Models with Mixture-of-Experts

extraction_data.innovation: Dense decoder-only Transformer model trained on the same dataset as the MoE variants, serving as a baseline for comparison. Uses per-layer relative positional bias and Gated Linear Units (GLU) in feed-forward sub-layers.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: High-quality dataset of 1.6 trillion tokens (filtered webpages, Wikipedia, conversations, forums, books, news)
extraction_data.application: Few-shot learning, language understanding, text generation
extraction_data.research_problem: Benchmarking dense models against sparsely activated MoE models for language understanding and generation
extraction_data.extension: Replaces standard positional embeddings with per-layer relative positional bias and uses Gated Linear Units (GLU) in feed-forward sub-layers.
extraction_data.paper_title: GLaM: Efficient Scaling of Language Models with Mixture-of-Experts

extraction_data.innovation: Efficient scaling of language models using a dense architecture, enabling competitive performance with reduced computational cost compared to sparse MoE models.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Large-scale text corpus (filtered and unfiltered webpages)
extraction_data.application: Natural language understanding (NLU), natural language generation (NLG), question answering, text classification
extraction_data.research_problem: Efficient scaling of large language models
extraction_data.extension: 2D sharding algorithm for large-scale training, dropout rate set to 0, and auxiliary loss for expert load balancing in MoE variants.

extraction_data.extension: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) layer with 64 experts, auxiliary loss for expert load balancing, and 2D sharding for large-scale training.

Achieves state-of-the-art performance on TriviaQA and other tasks with lower energy consumption than dense models like GPT-3.

properties.value: Zero-shot, one-shot, and few-shot learning tasks including open-domain question answering, common sense reasoning, natural language inference, reading comprehension, and cloze/completion tasks

properties.label: research_problem
properties.value: Efficient scaling of large language models with improved computational efficiency and predictive performance

properties.label: innovation
properties.value: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture enabling efficient scaling of language models by activating only a subset of parameters (experts) per input, reducing computational cost while maintaining performance.
